spec_match.rationale: Uses real `popover="auto"` and `popover="hint"` elements and observes their `toggle` events — exactly the spec surface area whose stacking rules are being rationalised in v149.

interactive.rationale: Four real trigger buttons drive four real popovers, and a live readout updates from the platform `toggle` event.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises `the diagram on the side shows the ancestor chain in real time`. The page only shows a flat open/closed list — there is no ancestor chain diagram.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers `open one of each`, but misses the marquee corner cases the spec change exists to fix: hint-inside-auto, auto-inside-hint, light-dismiss outside the chain, nested auto popovers with hint tooltips.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, the hint vs auto popovers are colour-coded via `--accent-amber` and `--accent-blue` borders, button override sets background + color together.

openQuestions.title: Add the ancestor-chain diagram the blurb promises
openQuestions.detail: Build a small inline-SVG (or pure-DOM) tree that, for each open popover, walks `popover.popoverInvoker` and ancestors to plot the chain. The blurb explicitly names this affordance and it is what would actually communicate the new stacking model — without it the demo collapses to a four-button toy.

openQuestions.title: Show the nested auto/hint corner case
openQuestions.detail: Add a fifth popover that's a hint nested inside an auto — that combination is the one the v149 change was designed to make work. Currently four sibling popovers don't exercise nesting at all, and nesting is the whole point.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate light-dismiss behaviour
openQuestions.detail: v149's rule is `hints only dismiss when their own ancestor chain dismisses`. Add a `click outside the chain` test — currently the only dismissal path the user can trigger is opening another popover. The light-dismiss case is what authors get bitten by.

openQuestions.title: Add a v148-style fallback annotation
openQuestions.detail: On v148 (pre-change), opening a hint would dismiss an unrelated auto. The page asserts the new behaviour in prose but never gives the visitor a way to see/compare the old behaviour. A small `behaviour table: pre-v149 / v149` block (purely informational) would lock the contrast in.
